title: adenostemma lavenia var. rugosum
intro: Adenostemma lavenia var. rugosum, also known as the 'Hawaiian False Daisy' or 'Sea Lavender', is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Adenostemma lavenia var. rugosum include its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and alleviate skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
